text.title: langchain Core Rules
text.summary: Migrated testable rules for langchain. Verify and refine before relying on for production.

rule.statement: Use `langchain-core` only in production agents, not the full `langchain` package — core is stable; full langchain pulls 200+ transitive dependencies.
rule.rationale: Dependency bloat creates version conflicts and larger container images. Core contains everything needed for LCEL chains and structured output.

rule.statement: Use `.with_structured_output(PydanticModel)` instead of output parsers; structured output is enforced by the API, not fragile string parsing.
rule.rationale: String parsers break silently when the model changes its output format. Structured output fails with a clear error that is easier to debug.

rule.statement: Test chains by invoking each component individually before wiring with `|`; isolates bugs before they compound in full chain traces.
rule.rationale: LCEL `|` produces hard-to-debug stack traces when intermediate steps fail; error messages point to internal chain internals rather than your code.

rule.statement: Enable LangSmith tracing from day 1 on new projects; retrofitting observability into existing chains is painful.
rule.rationale: LangSmith traces every `|` step with full input/output. Without it, debugging unexpected chain outputs requires print-statement archaeology.

rule.statement: Disable LangSmith tracing (`LANGCHAIN_TRACING_V2=false`) in latency-critical paths; tracing adds ~30ms per chain call.
rule.rationale: At high throughput, 30ms per call compounds. In a 10-step chain, tracing adds 300ms — measurable in user-facing endpoints.

rule.statement: Store LangGraph state as flat JSON-serializable dicts — nested custom objects silently fail serialization in checkpoint backends.
rule.rationale: Checkpoint backends (PostgreSQL, Redis) require JSON serialization. Custom Python objects cause silent serialization failures; the state appears to persist but is actually lost on process restart.

rule.statement: Always set `max_iterations` on agents using `RunnableConfig` or graph loop bounds — the default is unlimited.
rule.rationale: `create_react_agent()` has no built-in iteration cap. Agents loop indefinitely on ambiguous tasks without custom `stop_sequence` or `max_iterations` config.

rule.statement: Use `with_structured_output(PydanticModel)` for routing decisions in conditional edge functions; return the next node name as a typed field.
rule.rationale: String parsing of free-text routing decisions fails silently when the model produces unexpected phrasing. Structured output enforces exact node name values.

rule.statement: Use `interrupt_before=["action_node"]` for nodes that perform irreversible actions (send email, write to DB, call payment API); never rely on agent self-restraint.
rule.rationale: Agents cannot undo executed side effects. A misrouted task dispatched to a worker that calls an external API is irreversible without a human checkpoint.

rule.statement: Use `Annotated[list, operator.add]` for accumulated results fields in SupervisorState; direct list assignment discards previous worker outputs.
rule.rationale: LangGraph merges partial state updates with the accumulator. Plain list assignment replaces the list on every update, causing earlier worker results to be lost.

rule.statement: Test tool routing explicitly: invoke the agent with a sample query, inspect which tool was selected, and verify it's the expected one.
rule.rationale: Tool selection errors compound silently through the rest of the pipeline.

rule.statement: Sanitize user input before injecting into prompts; validate retrieved content before including in RAG chains to prevent prompt injection.
rule.rationale: A malicious document in the corpus or a crafted user input can override agent instructions. Retrieved content must be treated as untrusted data, not trusted instructions.

rule.statement: Use `create_react_agent(model, tools, checkpointer=MemorySaver())` for simple agent tasks driven by Claude subagents.
rule.rationale: This covers the common case and requires minimal boilerplate.

rule.statement: For complex workflows, define a LangGraph `StateGraph` where each node is a Claude subagent call with a specific role (planner, executor, critic).
rule.rationale: This separates concerns and makes the workflow explicit and testable.

rule.statement: Route between nodes with `add_conditional_edges` based on structured output from the previous node.
rule.rationale: Structured routing prevents silent failures from ambiguous or unparseable outputs.

rule.statement: Use `interrupt_before` for human-in-the-loop checkpoints at high-risk action nodes.
rule.rationale: This prevents irreversible actions without human approval.

rule.statement: Chain observability through LangSmith by setting `LANGCHAIN_TRACING_V2=true` — every subagent call is automatically traced with full input/output.
rule.rationale: This provides visibility into the entire workflow for debugging and optimization.
